pages.title: LZB
pages.recognition: The cab shows a permitted speed plus target speed and distance information.
pages.meaning: Continuous cab signalling and speed supervision are active.
playerAction: Follow the permitted speed shown in the cab.
Use target speed and target distance together to plan braking.
Do not wait for a lineside signal before responding to an LZB target.
notes: PZB normally remains relevant before entry and after leaving LZB.

pages.title: Target speed and distance
pages.recognition: A lower target speed is shown with a distance countdown.
pages.meaning: The train must reach the target speed by the target point.
playerAction: Begin braking early enough to remain below the supervised curve.
Expect the permitted-speed indication to step down as the target approaches.

pages.title: Leaving LZB
pages.recognition: An end-of-LZB indication or acknowledgement request appears.
pages.meaning: Continuous supervision is ending and driving returns to lineside signals and PZB.
playerAction: Acknowledge when required by the vehicle.
Identify the next lineside signal before the transition completes.
Continue under PZB and normal signal rules.
pages.warning: The exact acknowledgement control and timing are train-specific.

pages.title: LZB brake intervention
pages.recognition: The train brakes after overspeed or failure to follow the supervised curve.
pages.meaning: LZB has enforced its movement or speed limit.
playerAction: Allow the enforced braking to complete as required.
Re-establish the current permitted speed and target before applying power.
